mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above the threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with 60-day forward returns showing a negative or zero mean during concurrent RSAFS and HOUST expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls increase aggregate wage income and consumer demand, creating immediate positive momentum for S&P 500 equities over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during disinflationary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms sustained economic expansion, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization indicate supply-side bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns when capacity utilization remains above 80%, or the signal shows no directional edge over a 3-year walk-forward test.
